text:
This study explores the educational philosophy of KH. M.A. Sahal Mahfudh and its applicability within the contemporary Islamic education landscape in Indonesia. Utilizing a qualitative library research methodology, the research analyzes Kiai Sahal’s primary works and relevant academic literature to synthesize his pedagogical contributions. The findings reveal a paradigm that is inherently integrative, transformative, and contextual. Central to his thought is the rejection of the dichotomy between religious and secular sciences, advocating instead for a unified curriculum. Furthermore, Kiai Sahal introduced a holistic character-building framework known as the Basic Values of Sholih Akrom (NDSA), which emphasizes moral integrity alongside intellectual competence. 

The study demonstrates that Kiai Sahal’s ideas remain highly relevant to current educational reforms in Indonesia, particularly in the development of integrative curricula and adaptive learning models that respond to modern challenges without abandoning traditional foundations. While his philosophy informs various contemporary policies and institutional practices, the research also identifies persistent obstacles to implementation, such as institutional resistance to change and resource limitations. Ultimately, this research contributes to the discourse on developing a responsive Islamic educational system that harmonizes traditional wisdom with the necessities of the modern era.
